message:
fix(ft-api): tensor_aminmax composes through min_dim/max_dim [bd-b0y1]

tensor_aminmax used to extract values, compute mins/maxs in plain
f64, and rebuild non-grad leaves — silently severing autograd
through both branches. PyTorch's torch.aminmax IS differentiable
through both via argmin/argmax routing.

Compose through tensor_min_dim + tensor_max_dim (already autograd-
aware via the tape). Discard the index outputs since aminmax
doesn't return them.

Adds aminmax_propagates_gradient_via_argmin_argmax_routing regression
test:
  x = [[1, 5, 3], [2, 4, 6]], dim=1
  → mins = [1, 2]  (argmin = [0, 0])
  → maxs = [5, 6]  (argmax = [1, 2])
  Under sum loss over (mins + maxs):
    grad = [[1, 1, 0], [1, 0, 1]]   (one entry per row gets each routing)

Closes frankentorch-b0y1. ft-api lib tests: 1162 passing (+1).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankentorch-ap0","title":"Align core FrankenTorch spec with actual workspace and local oracle assumptions","description":"The main FrankenTorch spec still uses the older simplified architecture line and treats legacy_pytorch_code/pytorch as always present. Update only the core repo-map/oracle wording so the normative spec matches the current checkout and crate layout.","notes":"Updated the top-level FrankenTorch spec so the architecture blueprint and crate-family list match the current workspace, and the PyTorch oracle path is documented as a typical optional local mirror instead of an always-present checkout.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-03-27T17:59:41.440546462Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T18:00:05.575773878Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T18:00:05.575137443Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-aqm","title":"Add compile-time exhaustiveness guard for DType match arms","description":"## Context
The stub/placeholder audit (2026-04-02) found 26 unreachable!() calls. Many exist because match arms on DType handle a subset of variants and use unreachable!() for the rest after an early validation check. This pattern is fragile: if a new DType variant is added, the early check might be updated but the match arm might not, causing a panic.

## Problem
The DType enum has 9 variants (F64, F32, F16, BF16, I64, I32, Bool, Complex64, Complex128). Functions that only handle a subset (e.g., floating-point types) use an early if-guard then unreachable!() in the catch-all. A better pattern is to use a helper that converts the subset to a sub-enum, making the match exhaustive at compile time.

## Plan
1. Create typed helper enums or use existing type-level dispatch patterns:
   - FloatingDType { F64, F32, F16, BF16 } with TryFrom<DType>
   - NumericDType { F64, F32, I64, I32 } with TryFrom<DType>
2. Convert the most dangerous unreachable!() sites (those in hot paths like dispatch and backward) to use these sub-enums
3. The match becomes exhaustive and the compiler catches missing arms on enum changes
4. Less critical sites (test helpers, edge cases) can keep unreachable!() with messages

## Acceptance Criteria
- At least the 14 unreachable!() sites in ft-dispatch are converted to exhaustive matches
- New DType variants cause compile errors (not runtime panics) in dispatch code
- All existing tests pass

## Dependencies
- Depends on: frankentorch-dwy (DenseTensor::storage panic fix) -- both touch DType handling
- Should be done AFTER the storage() refactor to avoid merge conflicts

## Reasoning
This is the highest-leverage defense-in-depth change. It converts 14 potential runtime panics into compile-time errors, which aligns with the DAC correctness doctrine and Rust's zero-cost abstraction philosophy.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:23:32.701520867Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T23:54:37.770060012Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T23:54:37.769695201Z","close_reason":"Replaced all 14 ft-dispatch DType/promotion unreachable!() sites with typed non-complex storage classification, added regression tests for complex rejection and BF16 fallback, and verified ft-dispatch tests plus workspace check/clippy via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankentorch-aqm","depends_on_id":"frankentorch-dwy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:24:13.645570811Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankentorch-awo","title":"Add cross_entropy label_smoothing, weight_norm, spectral_norm, random_split, Subset","description":"Implemented: cross_entropy_loss_with_smoothing (label smoothing parameter), weight_norm_decompose/reconstruct, spectral_norm, Subset (Arc-based), random_split. 12 new tests, all 2714 workspace tests passing.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:28:22.787747419Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-04T01:28:27.120996753Z","closed_at":"2026-04-04T01:28:27.120748252Z","close_reason":"Implemented and tested. 12 new tests, all 2714 workspace tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-b0y1","title":"tensor_aminmax severs autograd; composable through min_dim/max_dim","description":"tensor_aminmax at lib.rs:15031 computes both min and max along a dim. Output is non-grad leaves. PyTorch's torch.aminmax IS differentiable through both branches (gradient routes via argmin/argmax to the corresponding input indices).

tensor_min_dim and tensor_max_dim already exist and are autograd-aware (they delegate to the tape's max_dim / min_dim primitives). Compose tensor_aminmax through them — discarding the indices since aminmax doesn't return them.

Surfaced during /reality-check pass continuing from cummax/cummin (wkkw).","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Composed tensor_aminmax through tensor_min_dim + tensor_max_dim (autograd-aware via the tape's argmin/argmax-routing reductions). Discarded the index outputs since aminmax doesn't return them. Added aminmax_propagates_gradient_via_argmin_argmax_routing regression test. ft-api lib tests: 1162.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T22:00:02.671736419Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T22:01:54.970889419Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T22:01:54.968592025Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-b5of","title":"torch.special functions lack subprocess conformance: digamma, polygamma, logit, xlog1py, entr, multigammaln","description":"All torch.special.* functions in ft-api/lib.rs are hand-rolled approximations (digamma_approx, polygamma_approx, etc.) but lack subprocess conformance against scipy.special. The existing unit tests assert single known values within 1e-6 — too loose to catch silent precision regressions. Add subprocess conformance harnesses comparing each against scipy.special.{digamma, polygamma, logit, xlog1py, entr, multigammaln}. Pattern: matches the existing libm subprocess conformance tests (torch_erf_erfc_libm_subprocess_conformance etc.) — ctypes-from-libm or scipy-via-numpy oracle, ULP-tolerant comparison.","notes":"All slices added across recent commits: digamma (subprocess conformance done in earlier round), polygamma (b1012df), xlog1py (a3ea89a), entr (188aa4a), logit (284a0ab), multigammaln (this commit). Each test fail-loud against scipy.special with ULP / relative-tolerance comparators. Total ~150 inputs covered across the six harnesses.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-29T20:07:12.196533219Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T21:51:45.349039993Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T21:51:45.345698845Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-b6n","title":"Audit ft-data loading path for latent correctness bugs","description":"## Context
The explicit parity backlog is empty, but we still need fresh-eyes subsystem audits to find latent correctness bugs hidden behind current green status. Start with ft-data and trace its batching/shuffle/drop_last behavior through tests and adjacent crate usage.

## Goal
Identify and fix any obvious correctness bug in the ft-data loading path, then verify with focused tests and workspace gates.

## Acceptance
- trace ft-data end-to-end
- fix at least one high-confidence correctness bug if found
- add/adjust tests proving the fix
- verify with rch cargo check/clippy/tests","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:10:45.588213207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-04T01:20:40.140473566Z","closed_at":"2026-04-04T01:20:40.140223743Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
